name: Root
description: HermesP2P Protocol Alignment — Phase 1 Root Contract. Defines the canonical type system, error handling conventions, pipeline envelope progression, serialization boundaries, constants conventions, and module independence rules for the HermesP2P peer-to-peer encrypted messaging protocol. This root contract establishes shared vocabulary and invariants consumed by all six sub-components: crypto_verify, discovery_module, integration, padding_module, pseudonyms_module, and ttl_module. All leaf modules (padding, ttl, pseudonyms, crypto_verify, discovery) are independently testable and do NOT import each other — only the integration module imports from leaf modules.

types.name: str
types.description: JavaScript string type. UTF-16 internally, UTF-8 when encoded via TextEncoder.

types.name: int
types.description: JavaScript number used as an integer. Safe integer range: -(2^53-1) to 2^53-1.

types.name: float
types.description: JavaScript IEEE 754 double-precision floating point number.

types.name: bool
types.description: JavaScript boolean: true or false.

types.name: bytes
types.description: Uint8Array — raw byte buffer. Used for all binary data throughout the protocol: keys, signatures, padded frames, encoded messages.

types.name: any
types.description: Any JavaScript value. Used sparingly for DOM events, callback functions, and WebSocket frame data before type discrimination.

types.name: None
types.description: JavaScript undefined or null. Indicates a function returns no meaningful value.

types.name: Base64String
types.description: A string containing valid base64-encoded data per RFC 4648 standard alphabet (A-Z, a-z, 0-9, +, /) with = padding. Encoded/decoded via window.base64js (fromByteArray / toByteArray). All base64 in the protocol uses standard encoding with padding — never base64url.

types.name: SecretKeyBase64
types.description: A base64-encoded Ed25519 secret key (64 bytes decoded). TweetNaCl format: first 32 bytes are the raw seed/key material, last 32 bytes are the corresponding public key. Canonical naming convention: use 'SecretKey' not 'PrivateKey' to match TweetNaCl terminology. NEVER log this value.

types.name: PublicKeyBase64
types.description: A base64-encoded Ed25519 public key (32 bytes decoded). Used with TweetNaCl nacl.sign.detached.verify and nacl.box operations. Canonical naming: 'PublicKeyBase64' with 'Base64' suffix indicating encoded form.

types.name: SignatureBase64
types.description: A base64-encoded Ed25519 detached signature (64 bytes decoded). Produced by nacl.sign.detached(messageBytes, secretKey). Covers the pre-padding JSON bytes of the message — the signature is computed over the UTF-8 encoded JSON string BEFORE padding is applied.

types.name: ChannelId
types.description: A non-empty string identifying a channel. Used as HMAC-SHA256 input for pseudonym generation. UTF-8 encoded before cryptographic operations.

types.name: PseudonymBase64
types.description: A 44-character base64 string representing 32 bytes of HMAC-SHA256 output. Deterministic per (SecretKey, ChannelId) pair. Unlinkable across channels and across identities.

types.name: WebSocketUrl
types.description: A string containing a valid WebSocket URL starting with 'ws://' or 'wss://'. Used for peer connection addresses.

types.name: EpochMilliseconds
types.description: A JavaScript number representing milliseconds since Unix epoch (1970-01-01T00:00:00Z). Produced by Date.now(). Used for all timestamps in the protocol.

types.name: TTLSeconds
types.description: A positive integer representing time-to-live in seconds. Stamped on messages during the send pipeline. Values: direct=300, private=3600, public=86400.

types.name: CleanupFunction
types.description: A no-argument function that clears all timers or resources started by an initialization routine. Safe to call multiple times (idempotent cleanup).

types.name: MessageType
types.description: The three recognized message types in the HermesP2P protocol. Determines TTL defaults, pseudonym behavior, and signature verification policy.

types.name: ReceivePipelineResult
types.description: Outcome classification of processing a received frame through the receive pipeline. Used for logging, metrics, and testing.

types.name: FrameSizeEnum
types.description: The set of allowed fixed frame sizes in bytes. Every padded user message will have byte length equal to exactly one of these values.

types.name: SideEffectKindEnum
types.description: Categorized side effect types used in error handling conventions. Leaf functions produce 'none'; integration functions may produce 'logging' or 'network_call'.

types.name: FromField

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name or shortened pseudonym. For channel messages: first 16 characters of PseudonymBase64 via shortenPseudonym(pseudonym, 16). For DM/public: user's configured display name.

fields.name: pubKey
fields.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 public key. Present for direct and public messages. ABSENT (not set, omitted from JSON) for channel (private) messages to preserve pseudonymity. Absence signals to the receive pipeline that signature verification should be skipped.

types.description: Sender identity block attached to every message package. For channel (private) messages: name is a shortened pseudonym (16 chars) and pubKey is absent/undefined (omitted from JSON serialization). For direct and public messages: name is the user's display name and pubKey is present. The presence or absence of pubKey drives signature verification policy on receive.

types.name: MessagePackage

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique message identifier. UUID v4 or equivalent. Used for deduplication on receive.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Message type: 'direct', 'private', or 'public'. Determines TTL, pseudonym, and verification behavior.

fields.name: from
fields.description: Sender identity block. Modified by pseudonym stage for channel messages.

fields.name: message
fields.description: The message content. May be encrypted (for direct/private) or plaintext (for public). The signature is computed over this field's value.

fields.name: signature
fields.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 detached signature over the UTF-8 encoding of the 'message' field. Computed BEFORE padding. Channel messages ARE signed on send but verification is skipped on receive (from.pubKey absent).

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Message creation time via Date.now(). Used for TTL expiry computation and age-based ordering.

fields.name: ttl
fields.description: Time-to-live in seconds. Added by stampTTL stage. Values: direct=300, private=3600, public=86400.

fields.name: hops
fields.description: Hop count. Added by stampTTL stage alongside ttl. Tracks how many relay hops this message has traversed.

types.description: The full message object constructed during the send pipeline. Fields are progressively added: base fields at compose, ttl at stampTTL, pseudonym modifications at pseudonym stage, signature at sign stage. This object is JSON.stringify'd to produce the wire format before padding. The signature covers the 'message' field content (the encrypted or plaintext message body), NOT the entire package JSON.

types.name: MessageEnvelope

fields.name: rawFrame
fields.description: Raw binary frame as received from WebSocket (ArrayBuffer converted to Uint8Array). Present at receive stage entry. Preserved for rebroadcast (never re-encoded).

fields.name: unpaddedBytes
fields.description: Bytes after unpadMessage removes padding. Present after unpad stage.

fields.name: jsonString
fields.description: UTF-8 decoded JSON string from unpaddedBytes. Present after deserialize stage.

fields.name: parsedMessage
fields.description: Parsed MessagePackage object from JSON.parse(jsonString). Present after deserialize stage.

fields.name: paddedFrame
fields.description: Padded binary frame ready for broadcast. Present after pad stage on send path.

fields.name: pipelineResult
fields.description: Final outcome classification of receive pipeline processing.

fields.name: senderUrl
fields.description: WebSocket URL of the peer that sent this frame. Present on receive path.

types.description: Progressive accumulation type representing a message as it flows through send/receive pipeline stages. Each stage reads the minimum fields it needs and adds its output fields. Send order: compose → encrypt → stampTTL → pseudonym → package → sign → serialize → pad → broadcast. Receive order: receive → classify → unpad → deserialize → TTL check → dedup → verify sig → decrypt → display. The integration module maps between leaf function inputs/outputs and envelope fields.

types.name: PaddedFrame

fields.name: messageRegion
fields.description: Bytes [0, originalLength): the original unpadded message content copied verbatim.

fields.name: randomPaddingRegion
fields.description: Bytes [originalLength, frameSize-2): filled with cryptographically random bytes via crypto.getRandomValues(). May be zero-length if message exactly fills available space.

fields.name: lengthSuffix
fields.description: Last 2 bytes of the frame: original message length as big-endian unsigned 16-bit integer (0..65534). Read/written via DataView.getUint16/setUint16.

types.description: Logical structure of a padded frame (physically stored as a flat Uint8Array). Layout: [message_bytes (0..originalLength) | random_padding (originalLength..frameSize-2) | length_suffix (last 2 bytes, big-endian uint16)]. The frame size is always one of FRAME_SIZES: 1024, 4096, 16384, 65536. Random padding uses crypto.getRandomValues() exclusively.

types.name: PeerEntry

fields.name: url
fields.description: WebSocket URL of the peer (e.g., 'wss://example.com/ws').

fields.name: lastSeen
fields.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds of when this peer was last seen or added/updated.

fields.name: lastLatency
fields.description: Last measured round-trip latency in milliseconds, or null if not yet measured.

types.description: A single peer record stored in KnownPeersCache. Represents a known peer with its URL, last-seen timestamp, and optional latency measurement.

types.name: OptionalFloat
types.description: A float value or null. Used for optional latency measurements.

types.name: OptionalStr
types.description: A string value or null. Used for optional pubKey, channelId, and peer selection results.

types.name: OptionalInt
types.description: An integer value or null.

types.name: OptionalMessagePackage
types.description: A MessagePackage or null. Used when parsing may fail.

types.name: OptionalPeerEntry
types.description: A PeerEntry or undefined. Returned by KnownPeersCache.get() when URL is not found.

types.name: PeerEntryList
types.description: Array of PeerEntry objects. Returned by KnownPeersCache.getAll().

types.name: StrList
types.description: Array of strings. Used for peer URL lists, bootstrap nodes, etc.

types.name: FrameSizesArray
types.description: Frozen array of allowed frame sizes in ascending order: [1024, 4096, 16384, 65536]. Exported as FRAME_SIZES constant. Object.freeze() prevents mutation.

types.name: ConnectedPeerInfoList
types.description: Array of ConnectedPeerInfo objects. Used by selectPeerForRotation.

types.name: PeerIdentity

fields.name: pubKey
fields.description: Base64-encoded public key of the sender.

types.description: Identity block included in peer exchange messages, containing the sender's public key.

types.name: PeerExchangeRequest

fields.name: type
fields.description: Always 'peer_exchange_request'.

fields.name: from
fields.description: Identity of the requesting peer.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the request was created.

types.description: A peer exchange request message. Sent as plain JSON text frame over WebSocket. NOT padded, TTL-stamped, or signed — separate protocol layer.

types.name: PeerExchangeResponse

fields.name: type
fields.description: Always 'peer_exchange_response'.

fields.name: peers
fields.description: Array of peer WebSocket URLs to share (max 5).

fields.name: from
fields.description: Identity of the responding peer.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the response was created.

types.description: A peer exchange response message containing a list of known peer URLs. Sent as plain JSON text frame over WebSocket. NOT padded, TTL-stamped, or signed.

types.name: PeerExchangeMessage
types.description: Either a PeerExchangeRequest or PeerExchangeResponse. Discriminated by the 'type' field.

types.name: ConnectedPeerInfo

fields.name: url
fields.description: WebSocket URL of the connected peer.

fields.name: connectedAt
fields.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when this peer connection was established.

types.description: Information about a currently connected peer, including connection establishment time. Used by selectPeerForRotation to identify the longest-connected peer.

types.name: IsProtocolResult

fields.name: isProtocol
fields.description: True if the data is a valid peer exchange protocol message (type starts with 'peer_exchange_').

fields.name: parsed
fields.description: The parsed JSON object if isProtocol is true, null otherwise.

types.description: Result of checking whether raw WebSocket data is a peer exchange protocol message. Never throws — always returns a valid result object.

types.name: NetworkAPI

fields.name: getConnectedPeerCount
fields.description: Function: () => int. Returns current number of connected peers.

fields.name: getConnectedPeerUrls
fields.description: Function: () => StrList. Returns URLs of all currently connected peers.

fields.name: connectToPeer
fields.description: Function: (url: str) => void. Initiates a WebSocket connection to the given peer URL.

fields.name: disconnectPeer
fields.description: Function: (url: str) => void. Closes the WebSocket connection to the given peer URL.

fields.name: sendToPeer
fields.description: Function: (url: str, data: str) => void. Sends a text frame to a specific connected peer.

fields.name: broadcastRaw
fields.description: Function: (data: str) => void. Broadcasts a text frame to all connected peers.

types.description: Dependency-injected network API object passed to initializeDiscovery. Provides accessor functions for peer state without exposing internal WebSocket data structures. Constructed in client.js from network.js exports to avoid circular imports.

types.name: PeerMetadata

fields.name: lastLatency
fields.description: Measured latency in ms, or null/undefined to keep existing or default to null.

types.description: Optional metadata provided when adding a peer to the cache. All fields are optional with defaults.

types.name: UserConfig

fields.name: privKey
fields.description: User's Ed25519 secret key as Uint8Array (64 bytes, TweetNaCl format). NEVER log this value.

fields.name: pubKey
fields.description: User's base64-encoded Ed25519 public key (32 bytes decoded).

fields.name: name
fields.description: User's chosen display name.

types.description: User identity configuration containing cryptographic keys and display name. Available in module scope of messages.js via the configuration object.

types.name: Configuration

fields.name: user
fields.description: Current user configuration including keys and display name.

types.description: Application configuration object available in module scope. Contains user identity and other settings.

types.name: DefaultTTLs

fields.name: direct
fields.description: TTL for direct messages: 300 seconds (5 minutes).

fields.name: private
fields.description: TTL for private/channel messages: 3600 seconds (1 hour).

fields.name: public
fields.description: TTL for public broadcast messages: 86400 seconds (24 hours).

types.description: Frozen constant mapping MessageType strings to default TTL in seconds. Defined at module top level in ttl.js as DEFAULT_TTLS.

types.name: TTLMessage

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Message creation time as ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds.

fields.name: ttl
fields.description: Original total time-to-live in seconds, as stamped at creation time.

types.description: Minimal message shape required by TTL functions (isExpired, getRemainingTTL, getRebroadcastProbability). Must contain timestamp and ttl fields.

types.name: Timestamp
types.description: A timestamp value: either an epoch milliseconds number or an ISO 8601 date string. parseTimestamp normalizes both forms to epoch milliseconds.

types.name: WebSocketFrame
types.description: Data received from or sent to a WebSocket. Text frames arrive as strings; binary frames arrive as ArrayBuffer (converted to Uint8Array). The frame type discriminates protocol messages (text/JSON) from user messages (binary/padded).

types.name: HkdfParams

fields.name: name
fields.description: Always 'HKDF'.

fields.name: hash
fields.description: Always 'SHA-256'.

fields.name: salt
fields.description: Always empty Uint8Array (new Uint8Array(0)). Empty salt per HKDF spec defaults to hash-length zero bytes.

fields.name: info
fields.description: UTF-8 encoding of the constant string 'hermes-pseudonym'. Domain separation parameter.

types.description: Fixed HKDF parameters used internally by pseudonym generation. Not exported from pseudonyms.js — documented for contract completeness and auditability.

types.name: DerivedHmacKeyParams

fields.name: name
fields.description: Always 'HMAC'.

fields.name: hash
fields.description: Always 'SHA-256'.

fields.name: length
fields.description: Always 256 (bits).

types.description: Fixed parameters for the HMAC key derived via HKDF in pseudonym generation. Not exported — documented for contract completeness.

types.name: ModuleConstants

fields.name: INFO_STRING
fields.description: Domain separation string: 'hermes-pseudonym'.

fields.name: SALT
fields.description: Empty salt: new Uint8Array(0).

fields.name: IKM_LENGTH
fields.description: Number of bytes taken from Ed25519 secret key as input key material: 32.

types.description: Module-level constants for pseudonyms.js defined at the top of the file.

types.name: DiscoveryConstants

fields.name: MIN_PEERS
fields.description: Minimum desired connected peer count: 3.

fields.name: KNOWN_PEERS_MAX
fields.description: Maximum entries in KnownPeersCache: 50.

fields.name: ROTATION_INTERVAL
fields.description: Peer rotation interval in milliseconds: 300000 (5 minutes).

fields.name: DISCOVERY_INTERVAL
fields.description: Health check / discovery interval in milliseconds: 30000 (30 seconds).

types.description: Module-level constants for discovery.js. Defined at module top level.

types.name: TTLConstants

fields.name: DEFAULT_TTLS
fields.description: Frozen object: { direct: 300, private: 3600, public: 86400 }.

fields.name: BASE_REBROADCAST_PROBABILITY
fields.description: Maximum rebroadcast probability: 0.6. Decays linearly as TTL is consumed.

fields.name: CLOCK_SKEW_TOLERANCE_MS
fields.description: Maximum allowed clock skew in milliseconds: 30000 (30 seconds). Ages that compute as negative (future timestamps) are clamped to 0 when within this tolerance.

types.description: Module-level constants for ttl.js. Defined at module top level.

types.name: PaddingConstants

fields.name: FRAME_SIZES
fields.description: Frozen array: [1024, 4096, 16384, 65536]. Immutable.

fields.name: MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE
fields.description: Maximum message payload before padding: 65534 bytes (65536 - 2 byte length suffix).

fields.name: LENGTH_SUFFIX_SIZE
fields.description: Size of the big-endian uint16 length suffix: 2 bytes.

types.description: Module-level constants for padding.js.

types.name: VerificationResult

fields.name: valid
fields.description: True if and only if nacl.sign.detached.verify returned true with correctly decoded inputs. False in all other cases including exceptions.

types.description: Conceptual result of verifySignature — always a plain boolean. Documented as a struct for contract completeness. True means the signature is valid for the given message and public key. False means invalid, malformed, or any error occurred (fail-closed).

types.name: ErrorConvention
types.description: Error handling conventions used across modules. Leaf functions in the receive pipeline never throw on bad input — they return fail-safe values. Leaf functions in the send pipeline may throw to surface bugs. Integration layer catches and routes all errors.

functions.name: base64Encode
functions.description: Encode a Uint8Array to a base64 string using window.base64js.fromByteArray. Existing module-scoped function in crypto.js promoted to a named export. No functional or signature change. Part of crypto_verify component.

inputs.name: uint8Array
inputs.description: The raw bytes to encode.

error_cases.name: invalid_input_type
error_cases.condition: uint8Array is not a Uint8Array instance

error_data.message: Input must be a Uint8Array

error_cases.name: missing_base64js_global
error_cases.condition: window.base64js is undefined or does not have fromByteArray method

error_data.message: window.base64js global not available

preconditions: window.base64js global is available and provides fromByteArray function
uint8Array is a valid Uint8Array instance
postconditions: Returned string is valid base64 encoding of the input bytes per RFC 4648
base64Decode(base64Encode(input)) produces a Uint8Array with identical content to input (round-trip identity)

functions.name: base64Decode
functions.description: Decode a base64 string to a Uint8Array using window.base64js.toByteArray. Existing module-scoped function in crypto.js promoted to a named export. No functional or signature change. Part of crypto_verify component.

inputs.name: base64String
inputs.description: A valid base64-encoded string to decode.

error_cases.name: invalid_base64
error_cases.condition: base64String contains characters outside the base64 alphabet or has invalid padding

error_data.message: Invalid base64 string

error_cases.name: missing_base64js_global
error_cases.condition: window.base64js is undefined or does not have toByteArray method

error_data.message: window.base64js global not available

preconditions: window.base64js global is available and provides toByteArray function
base64String is a valid base64-encoded string
postconditions: Returned Uint8Array contains the decoded bytes from the base64 input
base64Encode(base64Decode(validBase64)) returns a string equal to the input (modulo padding normalization)

functions.name: verifySignature
functions.description: Verify an Ed25519 detached signature against a message string and public key. Decodes base64 signature and publicKey via base64Decode, encodes message via TextEncoder, then calls window.nacl.sign.detached.verify. Returns boolean. Entire function body wrapped in try/catch: any exception returns false (fail-closed). MUST NOT be called on channel messages — channel messages lack from.pubKey for pseudonymity; the integration layer must skip verification for channel messages. Part of crypto_verify component.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The message content string to verify. Will be UTF-8 encoded via TextEncoder.

inputs.name: signature
inputs.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 detached signature (64 bytes decoded).

inputs.name: publicKey
inputs.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 public key (32 bytes decoded).

error_cases.name: malformed_signature_base64
error_cases.condition: signature parameter is not valid base64

error_cases.name: malformed_publickey_base64
error_cases.condition: publicKey parameter is not valid base64

error_cases.name: wrong_signature_length
error_cases.condition: Decoded signature is not exactly 64 bytes

error_cases.name: wrong_publickey_length
error_cases.condition: Decoded publicKey is not exactly 32 bytes

error_cases.name: signature_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Signature does not match the message under the given public key

error_cases.name: non_string_input
error_cases.condition: Any parameter is not a string (null, undefined, number, etc.)

error_cases.name: missing_nacl_global
error_cases.condition: window.nacl is not available at runtime

preconditions: window.nacl global is available and provides nacl.sign.detached.verify function
window.base64js global is available for base64Decode
All three parameters are strings
This function MUST NOT be called on channel messages (channel messages lack from.pubKey)
postconditions: Returns true if and only if: signature base64-decodes to valid 64-byte Ed25519 signature, publicKey base64-decodes to valid 32-byte Ed25519 public key, and nacl.sign.detached.verify confirms the signature for the UTF-8 encoded message bytes
Returns false for any invalid, malformed, or mismatched inputs
Returns false on any thrown exception (fail-closed)
Never throws an exception to the caller
Never logs message content, private keys, or secret key material

functions.name: padMessage
functions.description: Takes an unpadded message as a Uint8Array and returns a new Uint8Array padded to the smallest canonical frame size (1024, 4096, 16384, 65536) that fits the message plus a 2-byte big-endian length suffix. Message copied to position 0, gap filled with crypto.getRandomValues(), original length written as big-endian uint16 in last 2 bytes. Part of padding_module component.

inputs.name: messageBytes
inputs.description: The raw message bytes to pad. Must be a Uint8Array with length <= 65534.

error_cases.name: message_too_large
error_cases.condition: messageBytes.length > 65534

error_data.message: Message too large for any frame size: maximum payload is 65534 bytes

preconditions: messageBytes is a Uint8Array instance
messageBytes.length <= 65534
crypto.getRandomValues is available as a global function
postconditions: Returned Uint8Array has length equal to one of FRAME_SIZES: 1024, 4096, 16384, or 65536
Returned frame size is the smallest FRAME_SIZES entry >= messageBytes.length + 2
result.slice(0, messageBytes.length) is byte-equal to messageBytes
Last 2 bytes encode messageBytes.length as big-endian uint16
Bytes in [messageBytes.length, result.length - 2) are filled by crypto.getRandomValues()
unpadMessage(padMessage(messageBytes)) is byte-equal to messageBytes (round-trip invariant)
Result is a new Uint8Array — no aliasing with input buffer

functions.name: unpadMessage
functions.description: Extracts the original message from a padded frame by reading the big-endian uint16 length suffix from the last 2 bytes and returning a slice copy of bytes [0, originalLength). Validates consistency of encoded length with frame size. Part of padding_module component.

inputs.name: paddedBytes
inputs.description: A padded frame previously produced by padMessage.

validators.error_message: Frame must be at least 2 bytes

error_cases.name: frame_too_short
error_cases.condition: paddedBytes.length < 2

error_data.message: Padded frame too short: must be at least 2 bytes to contain the length suffix

error_cases.name: corrupted_length
error_cases.condition: Decoded length from last 2 bytes exceeds paddedBytes.length - 2

error_data.message: Decoded message length exceeds available frame space — corrupted or invalid frame

preconditions: paddedBytes is a Uint8Array instance
paddedBytes.length >= 2
Big-endian uint16 from last 2 bytes must be <= paddedBytes.length - 2
postconditions: Returned Uint8Array has length equal to the big-endian uint16 decoded from the last 2 bytes
Returned bytes are a copy (slice) of paddedBytes[0..decodedLength) — no aliasing
If paddedBytes was produced by padMessage(m), result is byte-equal to m

functions.name: generatePseudonym
functions.description: Generates a deterministic, channel-specific pseudonym from an Ed25519 secret key and a channel identifier. Uses HKDF to derive a per-purpose HMAC-SHA256 key from the first 32 bytes of the secret key, then signs the UTF-8-encoded channelId. Returns a 44-character base64 string. Async (uses crypto.subtle). NEVER logs key material. Part of pseudonyms_module component.

inputs.name: privateKeyBase64
inputs.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 secret key (64 bytes decoded). Only first 32 bytes used as HKDF IKM.

validators.error_message: privateKeyBase64 must be a non-empty string

inputs.name: channelId
inputs.description: Non-empty string identifying the channel. UTF-8 encoded before HMAC signing.

validators.error_message: channelId must be a non-empty string

error_cases.name: missing_private_key
error_cases.condition: privateKeyBase64 is undefined, null, or empty string

error_data.message: privateKeyBase64 is required and must be a non-empty string

error_cases.name: private_key_too_short
error_cases.condition: Decoded privateKeyBase64 is less than 32 bytes

error_data.message: privateKeyBase64 must decode to at least 32 bytes

error_cases.name: missing_channel_id
error_cases.condition: channelId is undefined, null, or empty string

error_data.message: channelId is required and must be a non-empty string

error_cases.name: webcrypto_unavailable
error_cases.condition: crypto.subtle is not available

error_data.message: crypto.subtle is required but not available

error_cases.name: base64js_unavailable
error_cases.condition: window.base64js is not available

error_data.message: window.base64js is required but not available

error_cases.name: hkdf_import_failure
error_cases.condition: crypto.subtle.importKey rejects

error_data.message: Failed to import key material as HKDF key

error_cases.name: key_derivation_failure
error_cases.condition: crypto.subtle.deriveKey rejects

error_data.message: Failed to derive HMAC key from HKDF key

error_cases.name: hmac_sign_failure
error_cases.condition: crypto.subtle.sign rejects

error_data.message: Failed to sign channelId with derived HMAC key

preconditions: privateKeyBase64 is a non-empty string containing valid base64 data
Decoded privateKeyBase64 is at least 32 bytes long
channelId is a non-empty string
window.base64js global is available
crypto.subtle global is available
TextEncoder global is available
postconditions: Returns a 44-character base64 string representing 32 bytes of HMAC-SHA256 output
Return value is deterministic: identical inputs always produce identical output
Return value differs when channelId differs (unlinkability across channels)
Return value differs when privateKeyBase64 differs (unlinkability across identities)
No private key material is logged to console at any point

functions.name: shortenPseudonym
functions.description: Returns the first `length` characters of a base64-encoded pseudonym string for display. Clamps length to [0, pseudonymBase64.length] so it never throws on out-of-range values. Synchronous, pure function. Part of pseudonyms_module component.

inputs.name: pseudonymBase64
inputs.description: The full base64-encoded pseudonym string.

inputs.name: length
inputs.description: Number of characters to return. Defaults to 8. Clamped to [0, pseudonymBase64.length].

error_cases.name: missing_pseudonym
error_cases.condition: pseudonymBase64 is undefined or null

error_data.message: pseudonymBase64 is required and must be a string

error_cases.name: invalid_pseudonym_type
error_cases.condition: pseudonymBase64 is not a string

error_data.message: pseudonymBase64 must be a string

error_cases.name: invalid_length_type
error_cases.condition: length is provided but is not a number or not an integer

error_data.message: length must be an integer

preconditions: pseudonymBase64 is a string (may be empty)
length is an integer (or omitted, defaulting to 8)
postconditions: Return value length equals Math.min(Math.max(0, length), pseudonymBase64.length)
Return value is a substring starting at index 0 of pseudonymBase64
When called with default length on a 44-character pseudonym, returns an 8-character string
When length exceeds pseudonymBase64.length, returns the full string
When length is 0 or negative, returns an empty string

functions.name: parseTimestamp
functions.description: Parses a timestamp value into epoch milliseconds. Accepts number (returned as-is) or string (parsed via new Date(ts).getTime()). Throws on unparseable input to surface malformed data early. Part of ttl_module component.

error_cases.condition: ts is a string but new Date(ts).getTime() returns NaN

error_data.message: Invalid timestamp: unparseable date string

preconditions: ts is not null and not undefined
postconditions: Return value is a finite number representing epoch milliseconds
Return value is not NaN

functions.name: getDefaultTTL
functions.description: Returns the default TTL in seconds for a given message type. Looks up type in DEFAULT_TTLS constant. Throws for unknown types to surface integration bugs. Part of ttl_module component.

inputs.name: messageType
inputs.description: One of 'direct', 'private', or 'public'.

error_cases.name: unknown_message_type
error_cases.condition: messageType is not one of 'direct', 'private', 'public'

error_data.message: Unknown message type: <messageType>

preconditions: messageType is a non-empty string
postconditions: Return value is a positive integer
Return value is one of: 300, 3600, 86400
getDefaultTTL('direct') === 300
getDefaultTTL('private') === 3600
getDefaultTTL('public') === 86400

functions.name: isExpired
functions.description: Checks whether a message has exceeded its TTL. Computes age, clamps to 0 for clock-skew safety, returns age >= ttl. Fail-safe: returns true (drop) if timestamp or ttl is missing/invalid. Never throws. Part of ttl_module component.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Message object with timestamp and ttl fields.

error_cases.name: malformed_message_failsafe
error_cases.condition: message is missing timestamp or ttl, or parseTimestamp throws, or ttl is not a positive number

error_data.behavior: Returns true (fail-safe: treat malformed messages as expired)

postconditions: Always returns a boolean, never throws
Returns true if message.timestamp or message.ttl is missing or invalid (fail-safe drop)
Returns true if computed age >= message.ttl
Returns false if computed age < message.ttl
Future timestamps (negative raw age) are clamped to age=0, so message is not expired

functions.name: getRemainingTTL
functions.description: Returns seconds remaining before message expires, clamped to 0. Computes Math.max(0, message.ttl - age) with age clamped to 0 for clock-skew safety. Part of ttl_module component.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Message object with timestamp and ttl fields.

error_cases.name: invalid_timestamp
error_cases.condition: message.timestamp cannot be parsed by parseTimestamp

error_data.message: Propagates Error from parseTimestamp

error_cases.name: missing_ttl
error_cases.condition: message.ttl is undefined, null, or not a number

error_data.message: message.ttl must be a number

preconditions: message.timestamp is a valid Timestamp parseable by parseTimestamp
message.ttl is a non-negative number
postconditions: Return value >= 0
Return value <= message.ttl
If isExpired(message) is true, return value is 0
If message has future timestamp, return value equals message.ttl (age clamped to 0)

functions.name: getRebroadcastProbability
functions.description: Computes rebroadcast probability using linear decay from BASE_REBROADCAST_PROBABILITY (0.6) based on remaining TTL fraction: 0.6 * (remainingTTL / message.ttl). Fail-safe: returns 0.0 for any edge case. Never throws. Part of ttl_module component.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Message object with timestamp and ttl fields.

error_cases.name: any_error_failsafe
error_cases.condition: Any exception during computation

error_data.behavior: Returns 0.0 (fail-safe: do not rebroadcast on error)

postconditions: Return value >= 0.0
Return value <= 0.6 (BASE_REBROADCAST_PROBABILITY)
If message.ttl <= 0, return value is 0.0
If isExpired(message), return value is 0.0
If full TTL remaining (freshly created), return value is 0.6
Never throws — returns 0.0 on any error

functions.name: stampTTL
functions.description: Adds the ttl field to a message package based on message type. Mutates in-place: messagePackage.ttl = getDefaultTTL(messageType). Returns same object reference for pipeline chaining. Part of ttl_module component.

inputs.name: messagePackage
inputs.description: Mutable message package object to stamp.

inputs.name: messageType
inputs.description: One of 'direct', 'private', 'public'.

preconditions: messagePackage is a non-null object
messageType is one of 'direct', 'private', 'public'
postconditions: Return value is the same object reference as messagePackage (===)
messagePackage.ttl is set to getDefaultTTL(messageType)
All other fields on messagePackage are preserved unchanged
If messagePackage already had a ttl field, it is overwritten

functions.name: packageMessage
functions.description: Async function that builds, stamps, signs, serializes, and pads a message for broadcast. Steps: (1) build message object, (2) stampTTL, (3) pseudonym for channels or keep identity for DM/public, (4) sign, (5) JSON.stringify, (6) padMessage(TextEncoder.encode(json)). Returns padded Uint8Array. Part of integration component.

For type='private': from.name is a 16-character shortened pseudonym
For type='direct' or 'public': from.pubKey is present
Embedded message has a valid Ed25519 signature over message content

Binary: unpad → deserialize → TTL check → dedup → signature verify → decrypt → display. Rebroadcasts valid messages using original raw frame bytes. Part of integration component.

functions.name: FRAME_SIZES
functions.description: Exported frozen constant array Object.freeze([1024, 4096, 16384, 65536]). Defines canonical padded frame sizes. Immutable. Documented as a named export of padding_module. This is a constant, not a function.
postconditions: Returned array is frozen (Object.isFrozen() === true)
Array has exactly 4 elements: [1024, 4096, 16384, 65536]
Elements are in strictly ascending order

invariants: PILLAR 1 — CANONICAL TYPE DEFINITIONS: All shared types use consistent naming: 'PublicKeyBase64' / 'SecretKeyBase64' (matching TweetNaCl terminology), 'Base64' suffix for encoded strings, 'Bytes' or Uint8Array for raw byte buffers. Base64 is always standard RFC 4648 with padding — never base64url.

PILLAR 2 — ERROR HANDLING CONVENTION: Leaf functions in the receive pipeline (isExpired, verifySignature, isProtocolMessage, unpadMessage) return fail-safe values on bad input (true/false/null) rather than throwing. Leaf functions in the send pipeline (padMessage, generatePseudonym, getDefaultTTL, parseTimestamp) throw on invalid input to surface bugs early. The integration layer catches all exceptions and routes to console.warn (security drops) or console.error (actual errors). console.warn for: expired TTL, failed signature, missing pubKey with signature. console.error for: malformed frames, missing dependencies.

PILLAR 3 — PIPELINE ENVELOPE: The send pipeline order is: compose → encrypt → stampTTL → pseudonym (channels) → package → sign → serialize → pad → broadcast. The receive pipeline order is: receive → classify (protocol shortcut for text frames) → unpad → deserialize → TTL check → dedup → verify sig (if pubKey present) → decrypt → display. Each leaf function accepts minimum required input and returns specific output; the integration module maps between them via the MessageEnvelope progression.

PILLAR 4 — SERIALIZATION BOUNDARIES: User message wire format is binary: JSON.stringify(MessagePackage) → TextEncoder.encode() → padMessage() → Uint8Array sent as WebSocket binary frame. Peer exchange wire format is plain JSON text (no padding, no TTL, no signing). WebSocket binaryType MUST be 'arraybuffer' for all connections. Text frames are routed to protocol/discovery handler; binary frames to user message pipeline. Frame type discrimination happens first in onmessage.

PILLAR 5 — CONSTANTS CONVENTION: Each module defines its own constants at the top of the file. padding_module owns: FRAME_SIZES=[1024,4096,16384,65536], MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE=65534, LENGTH_SUFFIX_SIZE=2. ttl_module owns: DEFAULT_TTLS={direct:300,private:3600,public:86400}, BASE_REBROADCAST_PROBABILITY=0.6, CLOCK_SKEW_TOLERANCE_MS=30000. discovery_module owns: MIN_PEERS=3, KNOWN_PEERS_MAX=50, ROTATION_INTERVAL=300000, DISCOVERY_INTERVAL=30000. pseudonyms_module owns: INFO_STRING='hermes-pseudonym', SALT=new Uint8Array(0), IKM_LENGTH=32. Constants are frozen where applicable (Object.freeze).

PILLAR 6 — MODULE INDEPENDENCE: Leaf modules (padding.js, ttl.js, pseudonyms.js, crypto.js, discovery.js) do NOT import each other. Only the integration module (network.js, messages.js, client.js) imports from leaf modules. This ensures each leaf is independently testable and prevents circular dependencies. Discovery receives network capabilities via dependency injection (NetworkAPI object) rather than importing network.js directly.

The signature (Ed25519 detached via nacl.sign.detached) covers the 'message' field content string encoded as UTF-8 bytes — NOT the entire MessagePackage JSON. The signature is computed BEFORE padding is applied.

Channel messages (type='private') ARE signed on send (signature is computed over message content) but signature verification is SKIPPED on receive because from.pubKey is absent. The integration layer enforces this skip: if from.pubKey is not present, verifySignature is not called.

Messages with from.pubKey present but missing or invalid signature are always dropped (security invariant). Messages with from.pubKey present but empty signature field are dropped with 'pubkey_without_signature_dropped'.

Raw binary frames are rebroadcast as-is — the original bytes from the sender are forwarded without re-encoding, re-padding, or re-serialization. This preserves padding integrity and prevents relay nodes from introducing distinguishable re-padded frames.
event.preventDefault() is always called synchronously in handleMessageSubmit BEFORE any await expression to prevent form submission race conditions.

Private keys, secret keys, HKDF-derived key material, and decrypted message content are NEVER logged to any output channel (console.log, console.warn, console.error). Only message IDs and metadata may be logged.
All modules use ES6 import/export syntax with named exports only — no default exports, no CommonJS require/module.exports.
All exported functions have JSDoc comments with @param and @returns annotations.
Each module file is located at static/js/<module>.js and remains under 300 lines.
crypto.getRandomValues() is used for all cryptographic random bytes — never Math.random().
Date.now() is used for all timestamps (EpochMilliseconds).

For padded frames: the last 2 bytes always encode the original message length as big-endian unsigned 16-bit integer. For any Uint8Array m where m.length <= 65534: unpadMessage(padMessage(m)) is byte-equal to m (round-trip correctness).

For pseudonyms: generatePseudonym is deterministic for fixed (SecretKeyBase64, ChannelId) pairs. Different channelIds produce different pseudonyms. Different secret keys produce different pseudonyms for the same channel. HKDF info is always UTF-8 of 'hermes-pseudonym', salt is always empty Uint8Array.
For TTL: age is always clamped to >= 0 to handle clock skew (future timestamps). Rebroadcast probability decays linearly from 0.6 to 0.0 as remaining TTL fraction decreases from 1.0 to 0.0.

For discovery: KnownPeersCache uses Map-based LRU eviction by lowest lastSeen. Cache size never exceeds configured maxSize. Peer exchange messages are plain JSON — never padded, TTL-stamped, signed, or encrypted.
For crypto_verify: verifySignature NEVER throws — all exceptions caught internally, returns false (fail-closed). base64Decode(base64Encode(x)) === x for any valid Uint8Array x.
broadcastMessage in network.js accepts both string (backward compatibility) and Uint8Array/ArrayBuffer (new binary pipeline) without breaking existing callers.
requires: Web Crypto API (crypto.subtle) for HMAC-SHA256 and HKDF operations
crypto.getRandomValues() for cryptographic random byte generation
TextEncoder and TextDecoder globals for UTF-8 encoding/decoding
